design_philosophy.primary_tension: Licensed IP constraint: adapting an R-rated slasher film into family-friendly Nintendo content
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: The game is defined by the tension between Friday the 13th's horror franchise identity and Nintendo's content restrictions. This results in a campy, horror-themed action game where the dark tone of the source material is fundamentally compromised by the medium and publisher requirements. The game is 'infamous' for this incongruity.

progression.upgrade_system: Item collection (unused items in data suggest attempted resource/inventory system)
progression.unlock_mechanism: Camp map navigation with cave access; path-based progression through locations
replayability_hooks: Multiple areas to explore on camp map
Hidden details in environment (unused path tiles suggest originally planned secrets)

world_building_techniques: Enemy type selection based on location (Jason presence implied)
Pamela Voorhees cave as narrative anchor

notable_bosses: Pamela Voorhees (cave encounter implied by environment design)

environmental_storytelling: Camp map layout reveals safe and dangerous zones
Path shading mechanics (though imperfectly implemented) suggest navigation hazards
Pamela Voorhees' cave as thematic destination
Unused item graphics (whistle, vial) suggest originally planned survival/signaling mechanics
